completed_tasks.name: P0-P2 runtime, state, tree, find, tap, and MIUI accessibility bind stabilization

remaining_tasks.name: P3 swipe acceptance and dispatch-capability retention hardening on MIUI

blockers.description: Production GhostAccessibilityService can fall from enabled_connected/dispatchCapable=true to enabled_idle/dispatchCapable=false between runs on the target MIUI device.
blockers.workaround: Re-enable Ghosthand Accessibility through settings or the dev helper before live verification. /state and dumpsys must both be checked before relying on swipe/tap results.

human_actions_pending.action: Before continuing any live accessibility gesture verification, manually confirm Ghosthand Accessibility is enabled and bound again if /state reports enabled_idle or disabled.
human_actions_pending.context: The target MIUI device can drop Ghosthand out of a dispatch-capable state between runs. Resume work should begin by checking /state and accessibility settings, then restoring enabled_connected if needed.

decisions.decision: Use /state plus dumpsys accessibility as the machine-readable truth sources for MIUI accessibility readiness.
decisions.rationale: The device can diverge from UI assumptions and stale visual state. /state.accessibility and dumpsys together reliably show enabled, connected, and dispatch-capable conditions.

decisions.decision: Treat swipe probe scrollability and accessibility gesture dispatch as separate paths.
decisions.rationale: Shell/manual swipe on the real probe can move the probe, while Ghosthand accessibility /swipe can still fail. Acceptance and diagnosis must not conflate those paths.

decisions.decision: Gate /swipe on real framework dispatch capability instead of lifecycle-connected state alone.
decisions.rationale: Instrumentation showed the correct connected service instance can still have connectionId=-1 and no framework connection, causing dispatchGesture() to return false immediately.

next_action: Resume by collecting one clean run-to-degradation timeline for the production GhostAccessibilityService: restore enabled_connected, capture service/lifecycle/system logs, then watch for the first event where dispatchCapable is lost.
context_notes: P2 is complete on the real MIUI rooted phone: production accessibility bind is stable with typeAllMask plus FLAG_INCLUDE_NOT_IMPORTANT_VIEWS; /tree, /find, and /tap all work and TAP PROBE increments. P3 /swipe exists and builds, and the swipe probe now exposes a truthful scroll signal from real scrollY and top visible item. Manual shell swipe using real probe bounds changes the swipe probe signal, but Ghosthand accessibility /swipe still fails because dispatchGesture() can be called when the service has no usable framework connection. A focused diagnostic pass showed that the correct service instance was used, but connectionId could be -1 with no framework connection. A bounded readiness fix was then added so /swipe fails honestly with ACCESSIBILITY_UNAVAILABLE when dispatchCapable is false. The remaining work is no longer API shape or gesture-shape experimentation; it is MIUI connection-retention diagnosis for the production GhostAccessibilityService between runs.
